rationale: Both candidates correctly diagnose the two real bugs and X's analysis of orchestrator.py:1290 tracks the ground-truth reviewer's exact reasoning (both submit_initial/submit_update reject lists, so gating on already_submitted was wrong) concisely and lands on APPROVE, matching the actual outcome (two LGTMs). Y repeats the identical review verbatim three times (a generation artifact) and its REQUEST CHANGES verdict is miscalibrated against reality since all its findings are non-blocking; it also includes one weaker/misleading finding (implying the error-propagation isinstance check is a new regression when the same check existed pre-diff). Y's test-coverage suggestions are grounded but generic, slightly diluting precision and actionability relative to X's tighter, better-grounded review.
